questions.questionText: Anaphylaxis is a classic example of which type of hypersensitivity reaction?
questions.explanation: IgE-mediated mast cell degranulation causes Type I hypersensitivity.

questions.questionText: Goodpasture syndrome represents which type of hypersensitivity?
questions.explanation: Antibodies directed against fixed basement membrane (anti-GBM) is Type II.

questions.questionText: Serum sickness and systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE) primarily involve which hypersensitivity mechanism?
questions.explanation: Deposition of circulating immune complexes causes Type III hypersensitivity.

questions.questionText: The tuberculin (PPD) skin test reaction is mediated by:
questions.explanation: It is a classical Delayed-Type Hypersensitivity (Type IV) response.

questions.questionText: Which autoantibody is highly specific (but not sensitive) for Systemic Lupus Erythematosus (SLE)?
questions.explanation: Anti-dsDNA and Anti-Smith are highly specific markers for SLE.

questions.questionText: Sjogren syndrome is characterized by the destruction of:
questions.explanation: Lymphocytic infiltration destroys exocrine glands causing dry eyes and dry mouth.

questions.questionText: The primary target of the Human Immunodeficiency Virus (HIV) is the:
questions.explanation: HIV uses the CD4 molecule as its primary receptor to infect helper T cells.

questions.questionText: A defining opportunistic infection in AIDS that presents as severe pneumonia is caused by:
questions.explanation: Pneumocystis pneumonia (PCP) is a classic AIDS-defining illness.

questions.questionText: Which condition is characterized by amyloid deposition composed of AL protein?
questions.explanation: AL amyloid consists of immunoglobulin light chains produced by plasma cell dyscrasias.

questions.questionText: Hyperacute transplant rejection is mediated by:
questions.optionA: Donor T cells attacking host tissues
questions.optionB: Preformed host antibodies against donor antigens
questions.explanation: Pre-existing antibodies cause immediate thrombosis and graft necrosis.

questions.questionText: In Immunopathology, which statement is most accurate regarding the core definition and diagnostic concept?
questions.optionA: It reflects the most exam-relevant definition used in standard Pathology texts
questions.optionB: It is an obsolete definition no longer used in clinical practice
questions.optionC: It is used only in pediatric population and never in adults
questions.optionD: It has no diagnostic significance in NEET-PG pattern questions
questions.explanation: This tests foundational definition-level knowledge from standard Pathology references for Immunopathology. Subject: Pathology. Chapter: Immunopathology.

questions.questionText: The most important etiology/risk factor to suspect first in a typical Immunopathology scenario is:
questions.optionA: The common high-yield risk factor repeatedly asked in entrance exams
questions.optionB: A rare cause seen only in isolated case reports
questions.optionD: An etiology excluded by routine first-line workup
questions.explanation: NEET-PG and INI-CET focus on common, high-yield causes before rare differentials. Subject: Pathology. Chapter: Immunopathology.
